countries.name: Angola
countries.narrative_headline: Angola: GDP growth at 2.1%, inflation at 21.6%, credit rating S&P: B-, Moody's: B3, Fitch: B-.

countries.name: United Arab Emirates
countries.narrative_headline: The UAE faces extreme chokepoint exposure (1.000 — maximum) as the Hormuz crisis blocks its primary oil export route.

countries.name: Argentina
countries.narrative_headline: Argentina is emerging as a net energy exporter for the first time via Vaca Muerta shale development, making it a beneficiary of the $100+ oil environment.

countries.name: Australia
countries.narrative_headline: Australia is modestly impacted by the global energy shock.

countries.name: Azerbaijan
countries.narrative_headline: Azerbaijan benefits from the Hormuz crisis with BTC (Baku-Tbilisi-Ceyhan) pipeline exports to the Mediterranean secure and not Hormuz-dependent.

countries.name: Bangladesh
countries.narrative_headline: Bangladesh is heavily dependent on Gulf oil transiting the Hormuz Strait, making it acutely vulnerable to the current crisis.

countries.name: Bulgaria
countries.narrative_headline: Bulgaria: GDP growth at 3%, inflation at 3.6%, credit rating S&P: BBB+, Moody's: Baa1, Fitch: BBB.

countries.name: Brazil
countries.narrative_headline: Brazil is a net beneficiary of the current geopolitical environment as a geographically insulated net oil exporter.

countries.name: Canada
countries.narrative_headline: Canada is a net beneficiary of the oil price surge as a major energy exporter with non-Hormuz export routes.

countries.name: Switzerland
countries.narrative_headline: Switzerland maintains its traditional safe-haven status amid the global geopolitical crisis.

countries.name: Chile
countries.narrative_headline: Chile's economy is broadly stable with limited direct impact from the Hormuz crisis.

countries.name: China
countries.narrative_headline: China remains relatively insulated from the Hormuz crisis via diversified oil imports and Russian pipeline supply.

countries.name: Côte d'Ivoire
countries.narrative_headline: Côte d'Ivoire: GDP growth at 6.4%, inflation at 1%, credit rating S&P: BB-, Moody's: NR, Fitch: BB-.

countries.name: DR Congo
countries.narrative_headline: DR Congo: GDP growth at 5.3%, inflation at 8.8%, credit rating S&P: B-, Moody's: NR, Fitch: NR.

countries.name: Colombia
countries.narrative_headline: Colombia benefits from elevated commodity prices as a net oil and coal exporter.

countries.name: Czech Republic
countries.narrative_headline: The Czech Republic's economy is broadly stable with limited direct impact from the Hormuz crisis beyond energy cost pass-through.

countries.name: Germany
countries.narrative_headline: Germany's industrial recession is deepening as the energy shock compounds structural competitiveness challenges.

countries.name: Egypt
countries.narrative_headline: Egypt faces severe pressure from a triple threat: oil import costs at $100+/bbl, Suez Canal revenue uncertainty from regional conflict, and tourism disruption from the Middle East war.

countries.name: Spain
countries.narrative_headline: Spain faces GDP pressure from the energy shock as a net energy importer.

countries.name: Ethiopia
countries.narrative_headline: Ethiopia is in selective default (SD/Ca/RD) with a 2,700 bps political risk premium.

countries.name: Finland
countries.narrative_headline: Finland's economy is broadly stable with limited direct impact from the Hormuz crisis.

countries.name: France
countries.narrative_headline: France is asserting defense leadership with an unprecedented 10-warship naval deployment to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the largest French naval operation in decades.

countries.name: United Kingdom
countries.narrative_headline: The UK faces GDP headwinds from the energy shock as a net energy importer.

countries.name: Georgia
countries.narrative_headline: Georgia: GDP growth at 7.2%, inflation at 3.9%, credit rating S&P: BB, Moody's: Ba2, Fitch: BB.

countries.name: Ghana
countries.narrative_headline: Ghana: GDP growth at 4%, inflation at 16.6%, credit rating S&P: RD, Moody's: NR, Fitch: RD.

countries.name: Greece
countries.narrative_headline: Greece faces GDP headwinds from the oil shock and European market crash (~6%).

countries.name: Croatia
countries.narrative_headline: Croatia: GDP growth at 3.1%, inflation at 4.4%, credit rating S&P: A-, Moody's: A3, Fitch: BBB+.

countries.name: Hungary
countries.narrative_headline: Hungary: GDP growth at 0.6%, inflation at 4.5%, credit rating S&P: BBB-, Moody's: Baa2, Fitch: BBB.

countries.name: Indonesia
countries.narrative_headline: Indonesia is modestly impacted by the energy shock.

countries.name: India
countries.narrative_headline: India is heavily dependent on Gulf oil transiting the Strait of Hormuz, making it one of the most exposed large economies to the current crisis.

countries.name: Ireland
countries.narrative_headline: Ireland's economy is broadly stable with limited direct impact from the Hormuz crisis.

countries.name: Iran
countries.narrative_headline: Iran is in a state of wartime economic and political collapse.

countries.name: Iraq
countries.narrative_headline: Iraq faces severe economic pressure from the Hormuz crisis as an oil exporter with extreme chokepoint dependence (0.791).

countries.name: Israel
countries.narrative_headline: Israel is prosecuting a multi-front war with expanding scope.

countries.name: Italy
countries.narrative_headline: Italy faces GDP pressure from the energy shock as a significant net energy importer.

countries.name: Jordan
countries.narrative_headline: Jordan is caught in the crossfire of the Iran-Israel conflict with IRGC strikes hitting targets in the region.

countries.name: Japan
countries.narrative_headline: Japan faces dual pressure from the energy shock and deteriorating Northeast Asian security.

countries.name: Kazakhstan
countries.narrative_headline: Kazakhstan benefits from elevated commodity prices as a major oil and minerals exporter with non-Hormuz export routes (CPC pipeline to Black Sea, China pipeline).

countries.name: Kenya
countries.narrative_headline: Kenya: GDP growth at 4.8%, inflation at 4%, credit rating S&P: B, Moody's: NR, Fitch: B.

countries.name: South Korea
countries.narrative_headline: South Korea faces a dual threat: the energy shock from oil above $100/bbl and severely elevated North Korean tensions after Pyongyang fired 10 ballistic missiles in response to the US-Korea Freedom Shield exercise with 18,000 personnel.

countries.name: Kuwait
countries.narrative_headline: Kuwait is the most exposed Gulf state to the Hormuz closure with no pipeline alternative.

countries.name: Libya
countries.narrative_headline: Libya: GDP growth at 15.6%, inflation at 1.8%, credit rating S&P: B, Moody's: NR, Fitch: B.

countries.name: Sri Lanka
countries.narrative_headline: Sri Lanka: GDP growth at 5.00871930957159%, inflation at -0.42936004906973%, credit rating S&P: CCC+, Moody's: Caa1, Fitch: RD.

countries.name: Morocco
countries.narrative_headline: Morocco's economy is broadly stable with limited direct impact from the Hormuz crisis.

countries.name: Mexico
countries.narrative_headline: Mexico is moderately positioned in the current environment.

countries.name: Myanmar
countries.narrative_headline: Myanmar is effectively a failed state.

countries.name: Mozambique
countries.narrative_headline: Mozambique: GDP growth at 2.5%, inflation at 4.9%, credit rating S&P: CCC+, Moody's: Caa2, Fitch: CCC+.

countries.name: Malaysia
countries.narrative_headline: Malaysia is moderately positioned as a net oil and LNG exporter, benefiting from elevated energy prices.

countries.name: Nigeria
countries.narrative_headline: Nigeria benefits from the oil price surge as an Atlantic coast oil exporter with secure, non-Hormuz export routes.

countries.name: Netherlands
countries.narrative_headline: The Netherlands faces GDP pressure from the energy shock as a European economy dependent on energy imports after Groningen gas field closure.

countries.name: Norway
countries.narrative_headline: Norway is a primary beneficiary of the current energy crisis.

countries.name: New Zealand
countries.narrative_headline: New Zealand's economy is broadly stable with limited direct impact from the Hormuz crisis.

countries.name: Oman
countries.narrative_headline: Oman faces severe economic pressure from the Hormuz crisis with extreme chokepoint exposure (0.850).

countries.name: Pakistan
countries.narrative_headline: Pakistan is among the most vulnerable large economies to the Hormuz disruption, with heavy dependence on Gulf oil transiting the Strait.

countries.name: Peru
countries.narrative_headline: Peru's economy is broadly stable with limited direct impact from the Hormuz crisis.

countries.name: Philippines
countries.narrative_headline: The Philippines faces GDP pressure from energy costs as a significant net energy importer.

countries.name: Poland
countries.narrative_headline: Poland's economy is broadly stable with limited direct impact from the Hormuz crisis beyond energy cost pass-through.

countries.name: Portugal
countries.narrative_headline: Portugal's economy is broadly stable with limited direct impact from the Hormuz crisis beyond energy cost pass-through.

countries.name: Qatar
countries.narrative_headline: Qatar faces extreme economic disruption as the world's largest LNG exporter is effectively offline due to the Hormuz blockade.

countries.name: Romania
countries.narrative_headline: Romania's economy is broadly stable with limited direct impact from the Hormuz crisis beyond energy cost pass-through.

countries.name: Russia
countries.narrative_headline: Russia is emerging as an early economic winner from the Iran war amid the oil price surge.

countries.name: Saudi Arabia
countries.narrative_headline: Saudi Arabia is severely impacted by the Hormuz crisis despite being an oil producer — its primary export route is blocked.

countries.name: Senegal
countries.narrative_headline: Senegal: GDP growth at 6%, inflation at 2%, credit rating S&P: CCC+, Moody's: Caa1, Fitch: CCC+.

countries.name: Singapore
countries.narrative_headline: Singapore faces critical chokepoint exposure (0.940) as a major shipping/trade hub dependent on unimpeded maritime routes.

countries.name: Serbia
countries.narrative_headline: Serbia: GDP growth at 2.4%, inflation at 4.6%, credit rating S&P: NR, Moody's: NR, Fitch: BB+.

countries.name: Sweden
countries.narrative_headline: Sweden's economy is broadly stable with modest energy cost pass-through.

countries.name: Thailand
countries.narrative_headline: Thailand's GDP is under pressure from energy costs as a net energy importer.

countries.name: Turkey
countries.narrative_headline: Turkey faces GDP headwinds from oil costs and regional instability as a net energy importer bordering the Middle East conflict zone.

countries.name: Taiwan
countries.narrative_headline: Taiwan's economy is broadly stable with limited direct impact from the Hormuz crisis.

countries.name: Tanzania
countries.narrative_headline: Tanzania: GDP growth at 6%, inflation at 3.3%, credit rating S&P: NR, Moody's: B2, Fitch: NR.

countries.name: Ukraine
countries.narrative_headline: Ukraine continues under active military assault from Russia with large-scale drone and missile barrages — at least 10 killed in Kharkiv this week.

countries.name: United States
countries.narrative_headline: The US-Iran war enters its third week with 6,000+ targets struck and no ceasefire in sight.

countries.name: Uzbekistan
countries.narrative_headline: Uzbekistan: GDP growth at 6.8%, inflation at 9.1%, credit rating S&P: BB-, Moody's: NR, Fitch: BB-.

countries.name: Vietnam
countries.narrative_headline: Vietnam held general elections with 73.5 million voters electing 500 National Assembly members, providing political continuity.

countries.name: South Africa
countries.narrative_headline: South Africa's GDP is under pressure from energy costs as a net oil importer, compounding existing structural challenges from load-shedding.
